qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via downloadFrom with arbitrary header injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via the downloadFrom feature allowing arbitrary URL fetching. The advisory describes a ReDoS vulnerability via the extraHttpHeaders scope feature using crafted regex patterns in pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go:200. These are different features with different root causes: the finding addresses unrestricted outbound HTTP requests via downloadFrom, while the advisory addresses catastrophic backtracking in regex pattern matching for scope validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described ReDoS vulnerability. The advisory describes a ReDoS vulnerability in Gotenberg's extraHttpHeaders scope feature where user-supplied scope patterns are compiled using dlclark/regexp2 without a MatchTimeout set, allowing attackers to hang workers indefinitely with crafted regex patterns containing nested quantifiers. The only qualifying finding describes SSRF via the downloadFrom feature, which is a completely different feature and vulnerability class. The advisory root cause is in pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go:200 where scope patterns are compiled, while the finding's root cause is in the downloadFrom URL processing in pkg/modules/api/context.go.
evidence: Advisory states: 'Gotenberg uses dlclark/regexp2 to compile user-supplied scope patterns (gotenberg/pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go:200) with no MatchTimeout set'
Advisory vulnerability class: CWE-1333 (Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity)
Advisory impact: Denial of service via worker hang from catastrophic regex backtracking
Finding vulnerability class: SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
Finding code locations: pkg/modules/api/context.go (downloadFrom processing), pkg/modules/api/api.go (URL filter configuration)
Finding root cause: Empty allow/deny lists for downloadFrom URLs and unrestricted extraHttpHeaders injection
No overlap in vulnerable code paths: Advisory targets scope regex compilation in chromium routes; finding targets downloadFrom URL fetching in API context middleware
